meta_title: For Students: Claude Code skills setup to Solve no version control (Advanced Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to Claude Code skills setup and overcome no version control. A Advanced-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Claude Code Setup: Solve No Version Control (Student Guide)
content.seo_description: Master Claude Code and Antigravity to solve version control issues. A high-impact checklist for students building their first production-ready AI applications.
content.answer_block: Version control is the bedrock of professional engineering. By integrating Claude Code with Antigravity, you automate branch management, commit hygiene, and synchronization. This setup eliminates the chaos of 'final_v2_code.py' files, ensuring your portfolio projects are robust, traceable, and ready for real-world deployment on day one.
content.intro:
You’ve spent hours crafting the perfect logic, only to lose everything in a rogue directory or an accidental overwrite. The 'no version control' trap is the silent killer of student innovation. It separates the hobbyists from the engineers who actually ship.

But here is the turning point: you don't need a decade of experience to master the workflow. By combining the autonomous intelligence of Claude Code with the structural integrity of Antigravity, you can force your development environment to handle versioning for you. Let’s turn your chaotic local folder into a professional-grade codebase.

steps.title: Initialize Repository Intelligence
steps.description: Before writing code, establish a Git baseline. We use Antigravity to ensure the repository structure adheres to industry standards, preventing future conflicts.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current project directory and initialize a clean, professional Git repository with a robust .gitignore file suited for AI/LLM development.

steps.title: Configure Auto-Commit Hooks
steps.description: Manual commits are prone to human error. Use Claude Code to configure pre-commit hooks that validate your code quality before a commit is finalized.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a set of pre-commit hooks that lint my Python code and check for sensitive API keys before allowing a git commit.

steps.title: Implement Branching Strategy
steps.description: Never work on 'main'. Define a workflow where features are developed in isolated branches, keeping your production code stable.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Explain and implement a simplified Git-flow branching strategy for a solo developer building a SaaS MVP.

steps.title: Automate Commit Documentation
steps.description: Clear commit messages are essential for your portfolio. Use Claude to analyze your diffs and generate semantic commit messages.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the current diff and write a conventional commit message that clearly explains the change and its impact on the system architecture.

steps.title: Sync and Remote Backup
steps.description: Ensure your local progress is mirrored securely. We link your local environment to a remote origin to prevent data loss.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Sync this local repository with a new private GitHub repository and ensure all remote tracking branches are correctly mapped.
steps.claude_code_command: claude remote --add origin <url> && git push -u origin main

pro_tips: Always use atomic commits: one logical change per commit to make debugging easier if you break the build.
Use 'git stash' before switching branches if Claude Code detects an uncommitted state to avoid losing context.
Treat your .gitignore as a security tool: never commit environment variables or local database files.
Tag your releases using semantic versioning (e.g., v1.0.0) so recruiters can see your progress milestones.
Review your Git log weekly; if it looks like a mess, use an interactive rebase to clean it up before showing it off.
content.next_steps: Once your version control is automated, start deploying your app to a cloud provider like Vercel or Railway to complete the full CI/CD loop.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting poor version control hold back your technical growth. Master these tools and start building with the confidence of a lead engineer. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
